utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey sean nice to see you i heard you that you have some back pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it's nice to see you too it's been a while

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah you know i had some back surgery and for some reason i always had lower

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: left back pain when was your back surgery

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it was in november of two thousand twenty

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: was that long ago

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow what happened what did they do microdiscectomy my disk broke off and just wrapped around and connected to my spinal thecal sac sounds horrible it's not the poop sac though

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's good to know what kind of symptoms did you have with that horrible pain down my leg in my back just horrible awful things that you can think of

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: horrible nerve pain it sounds like

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i think so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay did they give you any meds for that they did i was highly highly dosed up on gabapentin

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: was that hard to withdraw from

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah it was terrible sounds like yeah there is certainly take too much of that and then stop suddenly you can definitely have some some issues okay so you have left side low back pain

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does anything make it better or worse so my beautiful significant other always tells me to stretch and it always works

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: she sounds smart

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah she is so stretching helps okay what about any physical activities do they make it worse

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a lot of bending over

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: honestly it it just happens

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you know randomly

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: randomly okay so it hurt on the right side as well or just the left

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: depends on the day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is it right around where you had surgery

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: generally

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay did you try icing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm did you try any pain meds

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what kind of pain meds did you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ibuprofen eight hundred milligrams a day

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's quite a high dose okay let me do a quick physical exam on your back here if you bend forward does that hurt

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not right now but normally it would

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so no pain with flexion how about if you stand back up

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay no pain with extension either does it hurt while you're just standing here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so pain when you're weightbearing does it hurt when i push right here on the left side

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: pain with palpation to the left side around the l5 how about on the right side

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no pain with palpation to the right side okay i'm just gon na push down on your feet can you push back up on my hands

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright dorsiflexion is normal looks like that you have normal range of motion so that's good any pain down your legs

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great no nerve pain alright so we took an x-ray when you came in it looks like you do n't have any fractures or anything on the spinal bone that's good you can see where you had surgery it looks like everything has healed properly there so that's good news so what i think you did is you have a a muscle pull there from having a misalignment in your spine so what i'm gon na do is i'll prescribe some physical therapy for you you have great physical therapy here at the va so you can just come right to our physical therapist and that should help have you have you been there before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how do you feel about it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i really go to the dentist

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: got it okay so i'm also gon na prescribe you some meloxicam instead of the ibuprofen that might help a little bit more with some of the pain management

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: is there any chance i can get some vicodin

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lem me check your record quick for for pain management it's kind of a strong drug for vicodin and we do n't really like to prescribe that unless it's totally necessary so why do n't we start with the meloxicam and i also write a referral to pain management and they can talk to you a little bit about some of your pain and maybe not using narcotics to manage it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: how about some demerol

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmmm sure i'll prescribe demerol

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah you're welcome any other problems going on today you want to talk about

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright well it was nice to see you today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's always nice to see you as well

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

detail_list.summary: Sean Phillips is a 35-year-old male presenting with left-sided low back pain.
Low back pain
The patient has a history of back surgery.
The doctor asks when the patient had his back surgery.
The patient had back surgery in November 2020.
The patient underwent a microdiscectomy in 2020 for a broken disc.
The doctor asks if the patient took any medications for his previous nerve pain.
The patient reports taking high-dose gabapentin for nerve pain prior to his surgery.
The doctor asks what makes the pain better or worse.
The patient reports that stretching helps his pain.
The doctor asks if physical activities worsen the pain.
The patient reports that bending over worsens his pain.
The doctor asks if the pain occurs on the right side as well as the left.
The patient reports that the pain location varies by day.
The doctor asks if the pain is localized to the surgical site.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried icing the area.
The patient denies having tried icing the area.
The doctor asks if the patient has tried any pain medications.
The patient is taking ibuprofen 800 mg daily.
The doctor performs a physical exam of the lumbar spine.
The doctor asks if bending forward causes pain.
The patient denies pain with flexion during the exam.
The doctor notes no pain with flexion.
The doctor asks if extension causes pain.
The doctor notes no pain with extension and asks if standing causes pain.
The patient reports pain while standing.
The doctor asks if palpation on the left side causes pain.
The patient confirms pain with palpation on the left side.
The doctor notes pain with palpation around L5 on the left and asks about the right side.
The patient denies pain with palpation on the right side.
The doctor tests the patient's dorsiflexion.
The doctor notes normal dorsiflexion and range of motion, and asks about nerve pain in the legs.
The patient denies nerve pain in his legs.
The doctor reviews the X-ray, which shows no fractures and a healed surgical site, and diagnoses a muscle pull due to spinal misalignment.
The doctor recommends physical therapy.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam.
The patient asks for a prescription for Vicodin.
The doctor refers the patient to pain management.
The patient asks for a prescription for Demerol.
The doctor agrees to prescribe Demerol.
